title: Mildly Peppery German Potato Hash
description: A peppery, flaky German dish featuring harissa spice, perfect for a brunch mood, made vegetarian-friendly and cooked to perfection in a binchotan-grill, then finished in an air fryer for a crispy texture.
instructions: Preheat the binchotan-grill to medium heat and the air fryer to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the diced potatoes, onion, harissa spice, salt, black pepper, and garlic powder, and toss to coat evenly.
Add the vegetable oil to the bowl and toss the potato mixture again to coat.
Grill the potato mixture in the binchotan-grill for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it is lightly browned and crispy on the outside.
Transfer the grilled potato mixture to the air fryer basket and cook for an additional 5 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through.
Remove the potato hash from the air fryer and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Serve the Mildly Peppery German Potato Hash hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Let it cool for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
